title: baccharis tola var. chrysophylla
intro: Baccharis tola var. chrysophylla, also known as 'Palo Amargo,' is a shrub-like plant species native to the Andean region of South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Baccharis tola var. chrysophylla include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support overall well-being and combat various health conditions.
